title: leptosiphon chrysanthus subsp. chrysanthus
intro: Leptosiphon chrysanthus subsp. chrysanthus, also known as the yellow bush lupine or bush lupine, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Baja California. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Leptosiphon chrysanthus subsp. chrysanthus include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to reduce inflammation and pain. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and teas, which can be made by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
